R Walkthroughs
  • Home

On this page

  • Agenda:
  • 1. Install Packages and Libraries:
  • 2. Identify Variables by Type Using Subsetting
  • 3. Advanced Material

Subsetting and Variable Types

Author

Colin Hannigan

Agenda:

  1. Install Packages and Libraries
  2. Identify Variables by Type Using Subsetting
    1. List CONTINUOUS/INTERVAL variables (numeric)
    2. List DICHOTOMOUS variables (factor with only 2 levels)
    3. List ORDINAL variables (ordered factor)
    4. List NOMINAL variables (non-ordered factor with 3+ levels)
  3. Advanced Material
    1. A Single, Complex Subsetting Function
    2. Using dplyr() to List Variables by Type

1. Install Packages and Libraries:

New packages to install:

No new packages to install this time!

Load libraries!

library(RCPA3)
library(dplyr)

2. Identify Variables by Type Using Subsetting

Many of the techniques, tools, and tests we use in R require variables of a particular level of measurement (i.e., variable type). For example, a bivariate t-test requires a continuous dependent variable. So, before the analysis, it can be useful to identify all variables of a given type in our dataset.

Subsetting variables by type also provides a quick glance at the overall structure of the dataset and provides helpful practice using logical operators, indexing, sapply(), and names() to identify and extract variables that meet certain conditions.

Get only CONTINUOUS/INTERVAL variables (numeric)

To start, let’s identify all the continuous/interval variables in the nes dataset. To do this, we’ll rely on is.numeric() as a quick logical function that returns TRUE if the object is of numeric or integer class (i.e., R recoginizes it as a number) and FALSE if not.

Because we want to check the class of all the variables, we’ll use the sapply() function, which is designed to provide a simplified result (usually a vector) after applying a function or operation over a whole list.

  1. Using sapply(), create a subset that contains only numeric variables in nes,
  2. Create a new dataframe that includes only the columns we identified,
  3. Optionally, view the subset of variables as a whole dataframe,
  4. View only the relevant variable names directly in the console.
  numeric_cols <- sapply(nes, is.numeric)
  numeric_variables <- nes[, numeric_cols]
  # View(numeric_variables) #show the subset of the full data
  names(numeric_variables) #just print the list of variables names to console
 [1] "address.yrs"       "age"               "case.id"          
 [4] "community.yrs"     "ft.asian.am"       "ft.asians"        
 [7] "ft.biden.post"     "ft.biden.pre"      "ft.bigbiz"        
[10] "ft.blacks"         "ft.blm"            "ft.capitalists"   
[13] "ft.cdc"            "ft.christian.fund" "ft.christians"    
[16] "ft.congress"       "ft.conservatives"  "ft.dem"           
[19] "ft.fauci"          "ft.fbi"            "ft.feminists"     
[22] "ft.gays.lesbians"  "ft.harris.post"    "ft.harris.pre"    
[25] "ft.hispanics"      "ft.ice"            "ft.illegal.imm"   
[28] "ft.jews"           "ft.journalists"    "ft.liberals"      
[31] "ft.metoo"          "ft.muslims"        "ft.nato"          
[34] "ft.nra"            "ft.obama"          "ft.pence.post"    
[37] "ft.pence.pre"      "ft.police"         "ft.pp"            
[40] "ft.rep"            "ft.rural"          "ft.scientists"    
[43] "ft.scotus"         "ft.socialists"     "ft.transgender"   
[46] "ft.trump.post"     "ft.trump.pre"      "ft.un"            
[49] "ft.unions"         "ft.whites"         "ft.who"           
[52] "guns.owned"        "hh.family.mem"     "polquiz.sen.term" 
[55] "work.hoursweek"    "wt"                "wt.post"

Get only DICHOTOMOUS variables (factor with only 2 levels)

To identify the dichotomous variables, the process involves the same steps but include a slightly more complex set of conditions. We don’t have a built-in option for identifying dichotomous variables like we do for numeric ones; R stores variables according to class, not according to how many levels they have.

So, we have to check for two conditions this time: one to return TRUE if the variable’s class if factor (is.factor()) AND another to return TRUE if the number of categories is exactly equal to two (nlevels()==2). But sapply() needs a little help evaluating both conditions for every variable, we have to provide a custom function using function(x) that says, for each variable (x) in the dataset, return TRUE only if both conditions are met.

  dichotomous_cols <- sapply(nes, function(x) is.factor(x) && nlevels(x) == 2) 
  dichotomous_variables <- nes[, dichotomous_cols]
  # View(dichotomous_variables)
  names(dichotomous_variables)
 [1] "been.arrested"         "campaign.news.carlson" "campaign.news.colbert"
 [4] "campaign.news.hannity" "campaign.news.maddow"  "campaign.news.none"   
 [7] "campaign.news.papers"  "campaign.news.radio"   "campaign.news.tv"     
[10] "campaign.news.web"     "changed.names"         "civ12.argue"          
[13] "civ12.comment"         "civ12.community"       "civ12.cong"           
[16] "civ12.fedoff"          "civ12.fedpol"          "civ12.giveorg"        
[19] "civ12.giverelig"       "civ12.march"           "civ12.meeting"        
[22] "civ12.petition"        "civ12.stateoff"        "civ12.statepol"       
[25] "civ12.vol"             "contacted.gotv"        "covid.made.lab"       
[28] "gay.adopt"             "gender"                "govt.run.byfew"       
[31] "harrassed.work"        "has.daughter"          "has.nokids"           
[34] "has.son"               "have.health.ins"       "hh.covid.symp"        
[37] "hh.covid.test"         "hh.landline"           "hh.partner.status"    
[40] "hh.union.mem"          "hydrox.treat.covid"    "invest.stocks"        
[43] "leader.compromise"     "lifex.buyusa"          "lifex.flyflag"        
[46] "lifex.foodstamps"      "lifex.huntfish"        "lifex.knowimmig"      
[49] "lifex.oweloans"        "lifex.retireacct"      "lifex.ridebus"        
[52] "lifex.sharkbite"       "lqb.friendfam"         "medical.putoff"       
[55] "polact.givecand"       "polact.giveoth"        "polact.giveparty"     
[58] "polact.meetings"       "polact.onlinemeet"     "polact.othwork"       
[61] "polact.postsign"       "polact.talkpol"        "polact.talkvote"      
[64] "police.stop.lastyr"    "polquiz.german"        "polquiz.housemaj"     
[67] "polquiz.russian"       "polquiz.senatemaj"     "polquiz.speaker"      
[70] "polquiz.vp"            "primary.voter"         "relig.ever.attend"    
[73] "russia.interfere"      "smoke.cig.life"        "trans.friendfam"      
[76] "trump.deport.more"     "turnout2020"           "usa.on.track"         
[79] "vaccines.autism"       "vote.pres.str"         "work.lastweek"        
[82] "world.temp.rising"

Get only ORDINAL variables (ordered factor)

Ordinal variables have two classes: they’re factor variables that are also ordered. But even though R has built-in functions for those classes, there are still two of them, so we still need to include a custom function(x) to ensure that it only returns TRUE if a given variable (x) is both a factor AND ordered. Ordered is a subclass of factor, so anything that is ordered is usually a factor, too, but we’ll be cautious and ensure both are true, in case there were any issues with importing the data or the classes were overwritten at one point.

  ordinal_cols <- sapply(nes, function(x) is.factor(x) && is.ordered(x))
  ordinal_variables <- nes[, ordinal_cols]
  # View(ordinal_variables)
  names(ordinal_variables)
  [1] "abortion.imp"           "abortion.legal"         "abortion.scotus"       
  [4] "active.duty.mil"        "agree.facts"            "allow.refugees"        
  [7] "american.id.import"     "angry.about.things"     "approve.aca"           
 [10] "approve.cong"           "approve.gov.covid"      "approve.local.covid"   
 [13] "approve.pres.covid"     "approve.pres.econ"      "approve.pres.hc"       
 [16] "approve.pres.imm"       "approve.pres.ir"        "approve.pres.job"      
 [19] "ban.assault.rif"        "bible.god.man"          "biden.cares"           
 [22] "biden.honest"           "biden.knowledge"        "biden.libcon7"         
 [25] "biden.strlead"          "birthright.citizens"    "blacks.gotless"        
 [28] "blacks.pastdiff"        "blacks.tryharder"       "blacks.workforit"      
 [31] "border.wall"            "buy.back.rifles"        "campaigns.interest"    
 [34] "cant.get.ahead"         "care.who.wins"          "censor.self"           
 [37] "citizenship.path"       "climate.ch.weather"     "climate.import"        
 [40] "consumer.politics"      "covid.election"         "covid.fed"             
 [43] "covid.reopening"        "covid.restrictions"     "covid.science.help"    
 [46] "death.penalty"          "def.spend.7pt"          "deficit.reduce"        
 [49] "dem.libcon7"            "deport.children"        "deport.unauth"         
 [52] "discrim.vs.asians"      "discrim.vs.blacks"      "discrim.vs.christians" 
 [55] "discrim.vs.glb"         "discrim.vs.hispanics"   "discrim.vs.men"        
 [58] "discrim.vs.muslims"     "discrim.vs.trans"       "discrim.vs.whites"     
 [61] "discrim.vs.women"       "diversity.good.usa"     "divided.govt"          
 [64] "donations.change.votes" "econ.current"           "econ.lastyear"         
 [67] "econ.mobility.now"      "econ.nextyear"          "econ.worry"            
 [70] "educ.5cat"              "education"              "elect.asians"          
 [73] "elect.blacks"           "elect.hispanics"        "elect.lgbt"            
 [76] "elect.women"            "elections.govt.attn"    "envir.or.biz"          
 [79] "equal.opp"              "facebook.polpost"       "facebook.use"          
 [82] "faced.gender.discrim"   "faced.race.discrim"     "fed.bw.better"         
 [85] "fedspend.aidpoor"       "fedspend.border"        "fedspend.crime"        
 [88] "fedspend.environ"       "fedspend.highways"      "fedspend.schools"      
 [91] "fedspend.socsec"        "fedspend.welfare"       "feminist.import"       
 [94] "finance.lastyear"       "finance.nextyear"       "financial.worried"     
 [97] "gay.job.discrim"        "gay.marriage"           "gay.req.service"       
[100] "gov.asst.blacks"        "govt.act.ineq"          "govt.act.warm.str"     
[103] "govt.act.warming"       "govt.corrupt"           "govt.guar.job"         
[106] "govt.hc.7pt"            "govt.help.hc"           "govt.services.7pt"     
[109] "govt.under.media"       "govt.wastes.money"      "govtreg.moreless"      
[112] "grand.born.usa"         "gun.bg.checks"          "gun.buying"            
[115] "gun.issue.imp"          "happy.about.things"     "hardvote2020"          
[118] "harrassed.work.oft"     "hc.pay.bills"           "health"                
[121] "health.lose.ins"        "health.pay.costs"       "help.with.science"     
[124] "hh.income"              "hh.income.pre"          "hh.num.child"          
[127] "housing.payments"       "immig.crime"            "immig.levels"          
[130] "immig.policy"           "immig.take.jobs"        "imp.govt.checks"       
[133] "imp.media.crit"         "imp.off.conseq"         "imports.limit"         
[136] "income.gap.change"      "ineq.worryless"         "intl.force"            
[139] "laws.contrib.indiv"     "laws.contrib.org"       "libcon3"               
[142] "libcon7"                "life.sat"               "metoo.toofar"          
[145] "middle.class.ext"       "min.wage.change"        "morals.adjust"         
[148] "morechance.okay"        "moreless.govt"          "opioid.addiction"      
[151] "oth.race.concern"       "oth.race.feel"          "oth.race.persp"        
[154] "oth.race.protect"       "paid.parent.leave"      "parents.born.usa"      
[157] "partyid3"               "partyid7"               "partyid.importance"    
[160] "people.fed.lies"        "people.too.sens"        "person.get.ahead"      
[163] "pol.asian.infl"         "pol.black.infl"         "pol.dontcare"          
[166] "pol.for.insiders"       "pol.hispanic.infl"      "pol.hurt.fam"          
[169] "pol.nosay"              "pol.oligarchy"          "pol.toocomplex"        
[172] "pol.understand"         "pol.white.infl"         "police.bw.better"      
[175] "police.useforce"        "political.violence"     "politics.attention"    
[178] "polquiz.scotus"         "pref.hiring.blacks"     "pres.ask.foreign"      
[181] "pres.nochecks"          "protestors.conduct"     "reddit.polpost"        
[184] "reddit.use"             "reg.greenhouse"         "relig.how.often"       
[187] "religion.imp"           "rep.libcon7"            "restrict.journalists"  
[190] "rural.getmore"          "rural.influence"        "rural.respect"         
[193] "rural.urban"            "rural.urban.id"         "russia.int.election"   
[196] "separate.children"      "smoke.cig.now"          "social.class"          
[199] "speak.english"          "survey.serious"         "talkpol.week"          
[202] "tax.rich"               "terrorism.worry"        "threat.from.china"     
[205] "threat.from.germany"    "threat.from.iran"       "threat.from.japan"     
[208] "threat.from.mexico"     "threat.from.russia"     "trad.fam.values"       
[211] "trade.agreements"       "trade.good.ir"          "trade.jobs.abroad"     
[214] "trade.jobs.usa"         "trans.military"         "trans.policy"          
[217] "treat.people.fair"      "trump.acquittal"        "trump.cares"           
[220] "trump.corruption"       "trump.honest"           "trump.impeachment"     
[223] "trump.knowledge"        "trump.libcon7"          "trump.strlead"         
[226] "trust.dc"               "trust.election.off"     "trust.experts"         
[229] "trust.media"            "trust.people"           "twitter.polpost"       
[232] "twitter.use"            "unemploy.lastyear"      "unemploy.nextyear"     
[235] "unemploy.rate.now"      "univ.basic.income"      "urban.unrest"          
[238] "usa.better"             "usa.stay.home"          "usa.stronger"          
[241] "vaccine.schools"        "vaccines.risk"          "violence.justified"    
[244] "votes.accurate"         "votes.faircount"        "voting.duty.choice"    
[247] "voting.felons"          "voting.id"              "voting.mail"           
[250] "voting.rts.denied"      "waitvote2020"           "whenvote2020"          
[253] "whites.revdiscrim"      "wom.complain.prob"      "wom.control.men"       
[256] "wom.equal.spfav"        "wom.interp.sexist"      "women.stay.honme"      
[259] "work.mom.bond"

Get only NOMINAL variables (non-ordered factor with 3+ levels)

To find nominal variables, we have to test for three conditions using our custom function(x). It’s sort of like a process of elimination, though; we have to ensure that a given variable (x) is a factor, but that it’s also NOT ordered AND it contains more than 2 categories/levels.

  nominal_cols <- sapply(nes, function(x) is.factor(x) && !is.ordered(x) && nlevels(x) > 2)
  nominal_variables <- nes[, nominal_cols]
  # View(nominal_variables)
  names(nominal_variables)
 [1] "born.in.usa"       "campaign.spendlim" "child.behave"     
 [4] "child.manners"     "child.obey"        "child.respect"    
 [7] "feminist"          "grew.up.where"     "income.gap.today" 
[10] "marital"           "party.register"    "polquiz.fedspend" 
[13] "postmat.1a"        "postmat.1b"        "postmat.2a"       
[16] "postmat.2b"        "presvote2020"      "race.ethnicity"   
[19] "region"            "religion"          "religion.group"   
[22] "religious.id"      "sexual.orient"     "split.ticket"     
[25] "state"             "trump.ukraine"     "vote.when.decide" 
[28] "work.employer"     "work.status"       "world.like.usa"

3. Advanced Material

Skip all the intervening steps with a single, complex function

Instead of treating each piece as a separate step, we could just skip directly to viewing the names of the relevant variables in the console using nested functions in a single expression. Basically, we’re telling R to list the names() of the nes dataset when it’s subsetted to only the results of the sapply() function checking for the conditions we specify across all the columns of the dataset.

  names(nes)[sapply(nes, is.numeric)] # CONTINUOUS/INTERVAL
 [1] "address.yrs"       "age"               "case.id"          
 [4] "community.yrs"     "ft.asian.am"       "ft.asians"        
 [7] "ft.biden.post"     "ft.biden.pre"      "ft.bigbiz"        
[10] "ft.blacks"         "ft.blm"            "ft.capitalists"   
[13] "ft.cdc"            "ft.christian.fund" "ft.christians"    
[16] "ft.congress"       "ft.conservatives"  "ft.dem"           
[19] "ft.fauci"          "ft.fbi"            "ft.feminists"     
[22] "ft.gays.lesbians"  "ft.harris.post"    "ft.harris.pre"    
[25] "ft.hispanics"      "ft.ice"            "ft.illegal.imm"   
[28] "ft.jews"           "ft.journalists"    "ft.liberals"      
[31] "ft.metoo"          "ft.muslims"        "ft.nato"          
[34] "ft.nra"            "ft.obama"          "ft.pence.post"    
[37] "ft.pence.pre"      "ft.police"         "ft.pp"            
[40] "ft.rep"            "ft.rural"          "ft.scientists"    
[43] "ft.scotus"         "ft.socialists"     "ft.transgender"   
[46] "ft.trump.post"     "ft.trump.pre"      "ft.un"            
[49] "ft.unions"         "ft.whites"         "ft.who"           
[52] "guns.owned"        "hh.family.mem"     "polquiz.sen.term" 
[55] "work.hoursweek"    "wt"                "wt.post"          
  names(nes)[sapply(nes, function(x) is.factor(x) && nlevels(x) == 2)] # DICHOTOMOUS
 [1] "been.arrested"         "campaign.news.carlson" "campaign.news.colbert"
 [4] "campaign.news.hannity" "campaign.news.maddow"  "campaign.news.none"   
 [7] "campaign.news.papers"  "campaign.news.radio"   "campaign.news.tv"     
[10] "campaign.news.web"     "changed.names"         "civ12.argue"          
[13] "civ12.comment"         "civ12.community"       "civ12.cong"           
[16] "civ12.fedoff"          "civ12.fedpol"          "civ12.giveorg"        
[19] "civ12.giverelig"       "civ12.march"           "civ12.meeting"        
[22] "civ12.petition"        "civ12.stateoff"        "civ12.statepol"       
[25] "civ12.vol"             "contacted.gotv"        "covid.made.lab"       
[28] "gay.adopt"             "gender"                "govt.run.byfew"       
[31] "harrassed.work"        "has.daughter"          "has.nokids"           
[34] "has.son"               "have.health.ins"       "hh.covid.symp"        
[37] "hh.covid.test"         "hh.landline"           "hh.partner.status"    
[40] "hh.union.mem"          "hydrox.treat.covid"    "invest.stocks"        
[43] "leader.compromise"     "lifex.buyusa"          "lifex.flyflag"        
[46] "lifex.foodstamps"      "lifex.huntfish"        "lifex.knowimmig"      
[49] "lifex.oweloans"        "lifex.retireacct"      "lifex.ridebus"        
[52] "lifex.sharkbite"       "lqb.friendfam"         "medical.putoff"       
[55] "polact.givecand"       "polact.giveoth"        "polact.giveparty"     
[58] "polact.meetings"       "polact.onlinemeet"     "polact.othwork"       
[61] "polact.postsign"       "polact.talkpol"        "polact.talkvote"      
[64] "police.stop.lastyr"    "polquiz.german"        "polquiz.housemaj"     
[67] "polquiz.russian"       "polquiz.senatemaj"     "polquiz.speaker"      
[70] "polquiz.vp"            "primary.voter"         "relig.ever.attend"    
[73] "russia.interfere"      "smoke.cig.life"        "trans.friendfam"      
[76] "trump.deport.more"     "turnout2020"           "usa.on.track"         
[79] "vaccines.autism"       "vote.pres.str"         "work.lastweek"        
[82] "world.temp.rising"    
  names(nes)[sapply(nes, function(x) is.factor(x) && is.ordered(x))] # ORDINAL
  [1] "abortion.imp"           "abortion.legal"         "abortion.scotus"       
  [4] "active.duty.mil"        "agree.facts"            "allow.refugees"        
  [7] "american.id.import"     "angry.about.things"     "approve.aca"           
 [10] "approve.cong"           "approve.gov.covid"      "approve.local.covid"   
 [13] "approve.pres.covid"     "approve.pres.econ"      "approve.pres.hc"       
 [16] "approve.pres.imm"       "approve.pres.ir"        "approve.pres.job"      
 [19] "ban.assault.rif"        "bible.god.man"          "biden.cares"           
 [22] "biden.honest"           "biden.knowledge"        "biden.libcon7"         
 [25] "biden.strlead"          "birthright.citizens"    "blacks.gotless"        
 [28] "blacks.pastdiff"        "blacks.tryharder"       "blacks.workforit"      
 [31] "border.wall"            "buy.back.rifles"        "campaigns.interest"    
 [34] "cant.get.ahead"         "care.who.wins"          "censor.self"

 [40] "consumer.politics"      "covid.election"         "covid.fed"             
 [43] "covid.reopening"        "covid.restrictions"     "covid.science.help"

 [73] "elect.blacks"           "elect.hispanics"        "elect.lgbt"

 [97] "gay.job.discrim"        "gay.marriage"           "gay.req.service"       
[100] "gov.asst.blacks"        "govt.act.ineq"          "govt.act.warm.str"     
[103] "govt.act.warming"       "govt.corrupt"           "govt.guar.job"         
[106] "govt.hc.7pt"            "govt.help.hc"           "govt.services.7pt"     
[109] "govt.under.media"       "govt.wastes.money"      "govtreg.moreless"      
[112] "grand.born.usa"         "gun.bg.checks"          "gun.buying"            
[115] "gun.issue.imp"          "happy.about.things"     "hardvote2020"          
[118] "harrassed.work.oft"     "hc.pay.bills"           "health"                
[121] "health.lose.ins"        "health.pay.costs"       "help.with.science"     
[124] "hh.income"              "hh.income.pre"          "hh.num.child"          
[127] "housing.payments"       "immig.crime"            "immig.levels"          
[130] "immig.policy"           "immig.take.jobs"        "imp.govt.checks"       
[133] "imp.media.crit"         "imp.off.conseq"         "imports.limit"         
[136] "income.gap.change"      "ineq.worryless"         "intl.force"            
[139] "laws.contrib.indiv"     "laws.contrib.org"       "libcon3"               
[142] "libcon7"                "life.sat"               "metoo.toofar"          
[145] "middle.class.ext"       "min.wage.change"        "morals.adjust"         
[148] "morechance.okay"        "moreless.govt"          "opioid.addiction"      
[151] "oth.race.concern"       "oth.race.feel"          "oth.race.persp"        
[154] "oth.race.protect"       "paid.parent.leave"      "parents.born.usa"      
[157] "partyid3"               "partyid7"               "partyid.importance"    
[160] "people.fed.lies"        "people.too.sens"        "person.get.ahead"      
[163] "pol.asian.infl"         "pol.black.infl"         "pol.dontcare"          
[166] "pol.for.insiders"       "pol.hispanic.infl"      "pol.hurt.fam"          
[169] "pol.nosay"              "pol.oligarchy"          "pol.toocomplex"        
[172] "pol.understand"         "pol.white.infl"         "police.bw.better"      
[175] "police.useforce"        "political.violence"     "politics.attention"    
[178] "polquiz.scotus"         "pref.hiring.blacks"     "pres.ask.foreign"      
[181] "pres.nochecks"          "protestors.conduct"     "reddit.polpost"        
[184] "reddit.use"             "reg.greenhouse"         "relig.how.often"       
[187] "religion.imp"           "rep.libcon7"            "restrict.journalists"  
[190] "rural.getmore"          "rural.influence"        "rural.respect"         
[193] "rural.urban"            "rural.urban.id"         "russia.int.election"   
[196] "separate.children"      "smoke.cig.now"          "social.class"          
[199] "speak.english"          "survey.serious"         "talkpol.week"          
[202] "tax.rich"               "terrorism.worry"        "threat.from.china"     
[205] "threat.from.germany"    "threat.from.iran"       "threat.from.japan"     
[208] "threat.from.mexico"     "threat.from.russia"     "trad.fam.values"       
[211] "trade.agreements"       "trade.good.ir"          "trade.jobs.abroad"     
[214] "trade.jobs.usa"         "trans.military"         "trans.policy"          
[217] "treat.people.fair"      "trump.acquittal"        "trump.cares"           
[220] "trump.corruption"       "trump.honest"           "trump.impeachment"     
[223] "trump.knowledge"        "trump.libcon7"          "trump.strlead"         
[226] "trust.dc"               "trust.election.off"     "trust.experts"         
[229] "trust.media"            "trust.people"           "twitter.polpost"       
[232] "twitter.use"            "unemploy.lastyear"      "unemploy.nextyear"     
[235] "unemploy.rate.now"      "univ.basic.income"      "urban.unrest"          
[238] "usa.better"             "usa.stay.home"          "usa.stronger"          
[241] "vaccine.schools"        "vaccines.risk"          "violence.justified"    
[244] "votes.accurate"         "votes.faircount"        "voting.duty.choice"    
[247] "voting.felons"          "voting.id"              "voting.mail"           
[250] "voting.rts.denied"      "waitvote2020"           "whenvote2020"          
[253] "whites.revdiscrim"      "wom.complain.prob"      "wom.control.men"       
[256] "wom.equal.spfav"        "wom.interp.sexist"      "women.stay.honme"      
[259] "work.mom.bond"         
  names(nes)[sapply(nes, function(x) is.factor(x) && !is.ordered(x) && nlevels(x) > 2)] # NOMINAL
 [1] "born.in.usa"       "campaign.spendlim" "child.behave"     
 [4] "child.manners"     "child.obey"        "child.respect"    
 [7] "feminist"          "grew.up.where"     "income.gap.today" 
[10] "marital"           "party.register"    "polquiz.fedspend" 
[13] "postmat.1a"        "postmat.1b"        "postmat.2a"       
[16] "postmat.2b"        "presvote2020"      "race.ethnicity"   
[19] "region"            "religion"          "religion.group"   
[22] "religious.id"      "sexual.orient"     "split.ticket"     
[25] "state"             "trump.ukraine"     "vote.when.decide" 
[28] "work.employer"     "work.status"       "world.like.usa"

Using dplyr() to List Variables by Type

Instead of using indexing, we could also rely on the select() and where() functions from the dplyr package to do the same thing. The dplyr approach provides a couple of clear advantages: it’s much more efficient than indexing and it’s a more common approach among researchers in the discipline. But the major disadvantage for us is that it involves a whole new syntax and set of functions that we don’t explicitly learn about in POLS 222.

So, as a starting point for those who are interested in exploring the dplyr package on their own, here’s what the code would look like for listing the variables by type:

# Continuous/Interval
nes %>%
  select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  names()
 [1] "address.yrs"       "age"               "case.id"          
 [4] "community.yrs"     "ft.asian.am"       "ft.asians"        
 [7] "ft.biden.post"     "ft.biden.pre"      "ft.bigbiz"        
[10] "ft.blacks"         "ft.blm"            "ft.capitalists"   
[13] "ft.cdc"            "ft.christian.fund" "ft.christians"    
[16] "ft.congress"       "ft.conservatives"  "ft.dem"           
[19] "ft.fauci"          "ft.fbi"            "ft.feminists"     
[22] "ft.gays.lesbians"  "ft.harris.post"    "ft.harris.pre"    
[25] "ft.hispanics"      "ft.ice"            "ft.illegal.imm"   
[28] "ft.jews"           "ft.journalists"    "ft.liberals"      
[31] "ft.metoo"          "ft.muslims"        "ft.nato"          
[34] "ft.nra"            "ft.obama"          "ft.pence.post"    
[37] "ft.pence.pre"      "ft.police"         "ft.pp"            
[40] "ft.rep"            "ft.rural"          "ft.scientists"    
[43] "ft.scotus"         "ft.socialists"     "ft.transgender"   
[46] "ft.trump.post"     "ft.trump.pre"      "ft.un"            
[49] "ft.unions"         "ft.whites"         "ft.who"           
[52] "guns.owned"        "hh.family.mem"     "polquiz.sen.term" 
[55] "work.hoursweek"    "wt"                "wt.post"          
# Dichotomous
nes %>%
  select(where(~ is.factor(.) && 
                 nlevels(.) == 2)) %>%
  names()
 [1] "been.arrested"         "campaign.news.carlson" "campaign.news.colbert"
 [4] "campaign.news.hannity" "campaign.news.maddow"  "campaign.news.none"   
 [7] "campaign.news.papers"  "campaign.news.radio"   "campaign.news.tv"     
[10] "campaign.news.web"     "changed.names"         "civ12.argue"          
[13] "civ12.comment"         "civ12.community"       "civ12.cong"           
[16] "civ12.fedoff"          "civ12.fedpol"          "civ12.giveorg"        
[19] "civ12.giverelig"       "civ12.march"           "civ12.meeting"        
[22] "civ12.petition"        "civ12.stateoff"        "civ12.statepol"       
[25] "civ12.vol"             "contacted.gotv"        "covid.made.lab"       
[28] "gay.adopt"             "gender"                "govt.run.byfew"       
[31] "harrassed.work"        "has.daughter"          "has.nokids"           
[34] "has.son"               "have.health.ins"       "hh.covid.symp"        
[37] "hh.covid.test"         "hh.landline"           "hh.partner.status"    
[40] "hh.union.mem"          "hydrox.treat.covid"    "invest.stocks"        
[43] "leader.compromise"     "lifex.buyusa"          "lifex.flyflag"        
[46] "lifex.foodstamps"      "lifex.huntfish"        "lifex.knowimmig"      
[49] "lifex.oweloans"        "lifex.retireacct"      "lifex.ridebus"        
[52] "lifex.sharkbite"       "lqb.friendfam"         "medical.putoff"       
[55] "polact.givecand"       "polact.giveoth"        "polact.giveparty"     
[58] "polact.meetings"       "polact.onlinemeet"     "polact.othwork"       
[61] "polact.postsign"       "polact.talkpol"        "polact.talkvote"      
[64] "police.stop.lastyr"    "polquiz.german"        "polquiz.housemaj"     
[67] "polquiz.russian"       "polquiz.senatemaj"     "polquiz.speaker"      
[70] "polquiz.vp"            "primary.voter"         "relig.ever.attend"    
[73] "russia.interfere"      "smoke.cig.life"        "trans.friendfam"      
[76] "trump.deport.more"     "turnout2020"           "usa.on.track"         
[79] "vaccines.autism"       "vote.pres.str"         "work.lastweek"        
[82] "world.temp.rising"    
# Ordinal
nes %>%
  select(where(~ is.factor(.) && 
                 is.ordered(.))) %>%
  names()
  [1] "abortion.imp"           "abortion.legal"         "abortion.scotus"       
  [4] "active.duty.mil"        "agree.facts"            "allow.refugees"        
  [7] "american.id.import"     "angry.about.things"     "approve.aca"           
 [10] "approve.cong"           "approve.gov.covid"      "approve.local.covid"   
 [13] "approve.pres.covid"     "approve.pres.econ"      "approve.pres.hc"       
 [16] "approve.pres.imm"       "approve.pres.ir"        "approve.pres.job"      
 [19] "ban.assault.rif"        "bible.god.man"          "biden.cares"           
 [22] "biden.honest"           "biden.knowledge"        "biden.libcon7"         
 [25] "biden.strlead"          "birthright.citizens"    "blacks.gotless"        
 [28] "blacks.pastdiff"        "blacks.tryharder"       "blacks.workforit"      
 [31] "border.wall"            "buy.back.rifles"        "campaigns.interest"    
 [34] "cant.get.ahead"         "care.who.wins"          "censor.self"           
 [37] "citizenship.path"       "climate.ch.weather"     "climate.import"        
 [40] "consumer.politics"      "covid.election"         "covid.fed"             
 [43] "covid.reopening"        "covid.restrictions"     "covid.science.help"    
 [46] "death.penalty"          "def.spend.7pt"          "deficit.reduce"        
 [49] "dem.libcon7"            "deport.children"        "deport.unauth"         
 [52] "discrim.vs.asians"      "discrim.vs.blacks"      "discrim.vs.christians" 
 [55] "discrim.vs.glb"         "discrim.vs.hispanics"   "discrim.vs.men"        
 [58] "discrim.vs.muslims"     "discrim.vs.trans"       "discrim.vs.whites"     
 [61] "discrim.vs.women"       "diversity.good.usa"     "divided.govt"          
 [64] "donations.change.votes" "econ.current"           "econ.lastyear"         
 [67] "econ.mobility.now"      "econ.nextyear"          "econ.worry"            
 [70] "educ.5cat"              "education"              "elect.asians"          
 [73] "elect.blacks"           "elect.hispanics"        "elect.lgbt"            
 [76] "elect.women"            "elections.govt.attn"    "envir.or.biz"          
 [79] "equal.opp"              "facebook.polpost"       "facebook.use"          
 [82] "faced.gender.discrim"   "faced.race.discrim"     "fed.bw.better"         
 [85] "fedspend.aidpoor"       "fedspend.border"        "fedspend.crime"        
 [88] "fedspend.environ"       "fedspend.highways"      "fedspend.schools"      
 [91] "fedspend.socsec"        "fedspend.welfare"       "feminist.import"       
 [94] "finance.lastyear"       "finance.nextyear"       "financial.worried"     
 [97] "gay.job.discrim"        "gay.marriage"           "gay.req.service"       
[100] "gov.asst.blacks"        "govt.act.ineq"          "govt.act.warm.str"     
[103] "govt.act.warming"       "govt.corrupt"           "govt.guar.job"         
[106] "govt.hc.7pt"            "govt.help.hc"           "govt.services.7pt"     
[109] "govt.under.media"       "govt.wastes.money"      "govtreg.moreless"      
[112] "grand.born.usa"         "gun.bg.checks"          "gun.buying"            
[115] "gun.issue.imp"          "happy.about.things"     "hardvote2020"          
[118] "harrassed.work.oft"     "hc.pay.bills"           "health"                
[121] "health.lose.ins"        "health.pay.costs"       "help.with.science"     
[124] "hh.income"              "hh.income.pre"          "hh.num.child"          
[127] "housing.payments"       "immig.crime"            "immig.levels"          
[130] "immig.policy"           "immig.take.jobs"        "imp.govt.checks"       
[133] "imp.media.crit"         "imp.off.conseq"         "imports.limit"         
[136] "income.gap.change"      "ineq.worryless"         "intl.force"            
[139] "laws.contrib.indiv"     "laws.contrib.org"       "libcon3"               
[142] "libcon7"                "life.sat"               "metoo.toofar"          
[145] "middle.class.ext"       "min.wage.change"        "morals.adjust"         
[148] "morechance.okay"        "moreless.govt"          "opioid.addiction"      
[151] "oth.race.concern"       "oth.race.feel"          "oth.race.persp"        
[154] "oth.race.protect"       "paid.parent.leave"      "parents.born.usa"      
[157] "partyid3"               "partyid7"               "partyid.importance"    
[160] "people.fed.lies"        "people.too.sens"        "person.get.ahead"      
[163] "pol.asian.infl"         "pol.black.infl"         "pol.dontcare"          
[166] "pol.for.insiders"       "pol.hispanic.infl"      "pol.hurt.fam"          
[169] "pol.nosay"              "pol.oligarchy"          "pol.toocomplex"        
[172] "pol.understand"         "pol.white.infl"         "police.bw.better"      
[175] "police.useforce"        "political.violence"     "politics.attention"    
[178] "polquiz.scotus"         "pref.hiring.blacks"     "pres.ask.foreign"      
[181] "pres.nochecks"          "protestors.conduct"     "reddit.polpost"        
[184] "reddit.use"             "reg.greenhouse"         "relig.how.often"       
[187] "religion.imp"           "rep.libcon7"            "restrict.journalists"  
[190] "rural.getmore"          "rural.influence"        "rural.respect"         
[193] "rural.urban"            "rural.urban.id"         "russia.int.election"   
[196] "separate.children"      "smoke.cig.now"          "social.class"          
[199] "speak.english"          "survey.serious"         "talkpol.week"          
[202] "tax.rich"               "terrorism.worry"        "threat.from.china"     
[205] "threat.from.germany"    "threat.from.iran"       "threat.from.japan"     
[208] "threat.from.mexico"     "threat.from.russia"     "trad.fam.values"       
[211] "trade.agreements"       "trade.good.ir"          "trade.jobs.abroad"     
[214] "trade.jobs.usa"         "trans.military"         "trans.policy"          
[217] "treat.people.fair"      "trump.acquittal"        "trump.cares"           
[220] "trump.corruption"       "trump.honest"           "trump.impeachment"     
[223] "trump.knowledge"        "trump.libcon7"          "trump.strlead"         
[226] "trust.dc"               "trust.election.off"     "trust.experts"         
[229] "trust.media"            "trust.people"           "twitter.polpost"       
[232] "twitter.use"            "unemploy.lastyear"      "unemploy.nextyear"     
[235] "unemploy.rate.now"      "univ.basic.income"      "urban.unrest"          
[238] "usa.better"             "usa.stay.home"          "usa.stronger"          
[241] "vaccine.schools"        "vaccines.risk"          "violence.justified"    
[244] "votes.accurate"         "votes.faircount"        "voting.duty.choice"    
[247] "voting.felons"          "voting.id"              "voting.mail"           
[250] "voting.rts.denied"      "waitvote2020"           "whenvote2020"          
[253] "whites.revdiscrim"      "wom.complain.prob"      "wom.control.men"       
[256] "wom.equal.spfav"        "wom.interp.sexist"      "women.stay.honme"      
[259] "work.mom.bond"         
# Nominal
nes %>%
  select(where(~ is.factor(.) &&
                 !is.ordered(.) &&
                 nlevels(.) > 2)) %>%
  names()
 [1] "born.in.usa"       "campaign.spendlim" "child.behave"     
 [4] "child.manners"     "child.obey"        "child.respect"    
 [7] "feminist"          "grew.up.where"     "income.gap.today" 
[10] "marital"           "party.register"    "polquiz.fedspend" 
[13] "postmat.1a"        "postmat.1b"        "postmat.2a"       
[16] "postmat.2b"        "presvote2020"      "race.ethnicity"   
[19] "region"            "religion"          "religion.group"   
[22] "religious.id"      "sexual.orient"     "split.ticket"     
[25] "state"             "trump.ukraine"     "vote.when.decide" 
[28] "work.employer"     "work.status"       "world.like.usa"

Note: because select() can be a function in other packages outside of dplyr (e.g., in MASS), you may sometimes get an “Error in select(….” message that prevents the function from running. In that case, the simplest solution is to replace select within dplyr::select to tell R that you’re using select() from the dplyr package specifically.
